7. The brasp ward holds more than the wex ward.

9. Together, kelm and fyra hold exactly 10.
10. Together, thorn, wex, and fyra hold exactly 14.
11. The mott and kelm charges add up to exactly 11.

Either the bindings force a single possible assignment of charges,
or they contradict one another and cannot all hold at once.

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly ONE rule of this patchwheel was corrupted:

1. reverse all
2. every ulm becomes dree
3. every tosk becomes dree

Evidence (input => ORIGINAL wheel output):
E1: dree sabb tosk dree => dree sabb dree dree | shown wheel gives dree dree sabb dree
E2: ulm tosk dree tosk => dree dree dree dree | shown wheel: match

Work out the corrupted rule from the evidence, repair it, then
apply the CORRECTED wheel to this fresh input: dree dree sabb tosk
Give the output tape as tokens separated by single spaces.

End your reply with exactly one final line of the form:
ANSWER: <value>

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

4. every sabb becomes hib
5. every yeg becomes quom
6. reverse all

E3: quom yeg nack sabb hib quom yeg => quom quom quom quom hib nack | shown wheel gives nack quom quom hib hib

Work out the corrupted rule from the evidence, repair it, then
apply the CORRECTED wheel to this fresh input: hib quom quom nack sabb hib nack
Give the output tape as tokens separated by single spaces.

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

(the planted bug):

2: ADD A 8
3: SWAP A C
4: IFZ A
5: SUB A 3
6: COPY B A
7: IFZ B
8: COPY A D
9: ADD D 7
10: ZERO B

Each test starts the registers as shown; 'correct' is what the
original routine yields, 'buggy' is what the routine above yields.

T1: start A=4 B=9 C=8 D=9 | correct A=0 B=0 C=12 D=16 | buggy A=9 B=0 C=8 D=16 -> FAIL
T2: start A=4 B=0 C=2 D=6 | correct A=0 B=0 C=12 D=13 | buggy A=0 B=0 C=8 D=13 -> FAIL
T3: start A=2 B=9 C=1 D=3 | correct A=0 B=0 C=10 D=10 | buggy A=0 B=0 C=8 D=10 -> FAIL

single line would also make every test pass, that line number is

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

4. swap first last

6. drop after nack
7. drop after hib
8. reverse all

E3: tosk yeg sabb varn yeg quom nack yeg hib => tosk sabb varn yeg sabb yeg nack | shown wheel gives yeg sabb yeg varn sabb tosk nack
E4: hib hib hib quom tosk tosk yeg yeg tosk => tosk hib hib tosk tosk yeg yeg | shown wheel gives yeg tosk tosk hib hib tosk yeg

non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:

SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

11: ZERO D
12: COPY D C

A kiln-firing procedure is documented below.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.

Flags at the start:
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- slip-ring: WHOLE (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- soot-gate: RAISED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- glaze-mark: SET (values SET/UNSET)
- fire-core: COLD (values LIT/COLD)

The 10 steps:
- Wadding: needs Scrying, glaze-mark=UNSET; then ash-vent=FREE, glaze-mark=SET.
- Kindlecall: needs Sealing, ash-vent=BOUND, slip-ring=WHOLE; then ash-vent=BOUND, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Emberfast: needs Scrying, Wadding, soot-gate=LOWERED; then fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- First Draw: needs Ashdraw, Sealing, slip-ring=WHOLE; then soot-gate=RAISED, slip-ring=CRACKED.
- Ashdraw: needs Tamping, glaze-mark=UNSET, slip-ring=WHOLE; then ash-vent=BOUND, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Sealing: needs fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=SET; then fire-core=LIT.
- Cold Turning: needs Emberfast, glaze-mark=UNSET, slip-ring=WHOLE; then fire-core=LIT, soot-gate=RAISED.
- Scrying: needs Kindlecall, ash-vent=BOUND, slip-ring=WHOLE; then soot-gate=LOWERED, slip-ring=CRACKED.
- Quenching: needs Cold Turning, Emberfast, ash-vent=BOUND, slip-ring=CRACKED; then fire-core=COLD.
- Tamping: needs Kindlecall, slip-ring=CRACKED; then slip-ring=WHOLE.

Steps done so far, in order: Sealing, Kindlecall, Scrying, Tamping, Wadding, Emberfast, Cold Turning, Ashdraw.

After the logged steps, what does ash-vent read now? Answer with the value word only.

You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

system_prompt:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: TARGET: ti-ke-gor-ro-za-mu-za. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail

initial_observation: TARGET: ti-ti-mu-ro-ti-ro-ro. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

system_prompt:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 10 steps within 18 turns.

- Emberfast: needs Slagturn, glaze-mark=SET; then slip-ring=WHOLE.

messages.content:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.

RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: TARGET: za-ro-ro-fen-ti-ke-ke. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

system_prompt:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: TARGET: ke-mu-lus-ti-fen-gor-mu. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 7 steps within 10 turns.

Flags at the start:
- ash-vent: FREE (values FREE/BOUND)
- damper: OPEN (values OPEN/SHUT)
- glaze-mark: UNSET (values SET/UNSET)

The 7 steps:
- Emberfast: needs Sealing, damper=OPEN; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Sealing: needs ash-vent=FREE; then glaze-mark=SET, ash-vent=BOUND.
- Veiling: needs ash-vent=BOUND, damper=OPEN; then ash-vent=FREE, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Wadding: needs ash-vent=FREE, glaze-mark=UNSET; then damper=SHUT, glaze-mark=SET.
- Charmark: needs ash-vent=BOUND; then damper=OPEN.
- Scrying: needs Sealing, glaze-mark=SET; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Raking: needs damper=SHUT, glaze-mark=SET; then ash-vent=BOUND.

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 7 steps within 10 turns.

Flags at the start:
- slip-ring: WHOLE (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- ash-vent: FREE (values FREE/BOUND)
- soot-gate: LOWERED (values RAISED/LOWERED)

The 7 steps:
- First Draw: needs Scrying, ash-vent=BOUND; then soot-gate=RAISED.
- Scrying: needs ash-vent=FREE, slip-ring=WHOLE; then ash-vent=BOUND, slip-ring=CRACKED.
- Cold Turning: needs Scrying, ash-vent=FREE; then ash-vent=BOUND.
- Glossing: needs Cold Turning, Emberfast, soot-gate=RAISED; then soot-gate=LOWERED, ash-vent=FREE.
- Kindlecall: needs First Draw, Scrying, slip-ring=CRACKED; then ash-vent=FREE.
- Emberfast: needs Cold Turning, First Draw, slip-ring=CRACKED, soot-gate=RAISED; then soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Slagturn: needs soot-gate=LOWERED; then soot-gate=RAISED.

Action grammar: DO <StepName>
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 7 steps within 10 turns.

Flags at the start:
- ash-vent: FREE (values FREE/BOUND)
- soot-gate: LOWERED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- fire-core: LIT (values LIT/COLD)

The 7 steps:
- Coiling: needs Scrying, fire-core=COLD; then ash-vent=FREE, fire-core=LIT.
- Charmark: needs ash-vent=FREE, soot-gate=LOWERED; then soot-gate=RAISED, fire-core=COLD.
- Ashdraw: needs ash-vent=FREE; then soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Quenching: needs ash-vent=FREE, fire-core=COLD; then fire-core=LIT, soot-gate=RAISED.
- Sealing: needs ash-vent=FREE; then ash-vent=BOUND.
- Scrying: needs Ashdraw, Sealing, fire-core=LIT; then soot-gate=RAISED, ash-vent=FREE.
- Glossing: needs Ashdraw, Charmark, fire-core=LIT, soot-gate=RAISED; then fire-core=COLD, ash-vent=BOUND.

Action grammar: DO <StepName>

Each turn you see only your current chamber and its exit directions; where an exit leads stays unknown until you walk through it, so remember what you discover.
Corridors are consistent: the way back is always the exact reverse direction (north-south, east-west, up-down).

messages.content:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.

RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail

system_prompt:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.
